narration_text: Now we'll see how this caravan analogy helps us understand packet routing. Imagine a ten-car caravan traveling a hundred kilometers to its destination, but it has to pass through a toll booth, which represents a router. Look at the diagram on your slide — notice how the caravan is broken down into smaller parts, much like our data packets are divided into manageable bits. The toll booth, or router, examines each part and determines the best route forward. I want you to focus on the image and observe how the caravan's journey is similar to data transmission. As we analyze this further, let's think about this together and see how it applies to our discussion on packet routing, which we'll continue to explore in the next section.
